content:
You are working on an existing React Native (Expo) marketplace app called Heavyar.

IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT break existing working features
- Do NOT refactor unrelated code
- Keep current UI style and structure
- Only extend logic cleanly
- Ensure Arabic is default and English is supported (RTL/LTR must remain correct)

==================================================
PHASE 3.6 — USER ROLES + PROFILE SYSTEM
==================================================

1. Extend Firestore "users" collection:

Each user document must include:

- id (uid)
- name
- phone
- city
- role: "customer" | "provider"
- crNumber (string, optional)
- crVerified (boolean, default false)
- createdAt

2. Registration flow:

- Add role selection screen (customer / provider)
- If role = provider:
    - Show optional field: Commercial Registration Number
    - Validation:
        - If entered → must be exactly 10 digits
- Save role + crNumber to Firestore

3. Profile Screen (NEW or extend existing):

Editable fields:
- name
- phone
- city

If role = provider:
- show CR number field
- show CR status (verified / not verified)

Add:
- Save button
- Proper validation
- Loading + success states

4. Access control:

- Only provider can:
    - Add equipment
    - Edit equipment
    - Delete equipment

- If user is not provider:
    - Hide or disable these actions

==================================================
PHASE 3.7 — REQUEST SYSTEM (STABILIZE & COMPLETE)
==================================================

1. Firestore collection: "requests"

Structure:

- id
- equipmentId
- equipmentName
- customerId
- providerId
- status: "pending" | "accepted" | "rejected" | "cancelled" | "completed"
- createdAt

2. Create request:

From equipment details screen:

- Only logged-in users can create request
- When user clicks "Request":
    → create document in "requests"

3. Customer side:

Create screen:
- "My Requests"

Show:
- equipment name
- status
- date

4. Provider side:

Create screen:
- "Incoming Requests"

Show:
- equipment name
- customer info (if available)
- status

Provider actions:
- Accept → status = accepted
- Reject → status = rejected

5. Status handling:

Ensure:
- UI updates based on status
- No invalid transitions

==================================================
UX REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

- Keep UI clean and minimal
- Reuse existing components where possible
- Add loading indicators for async operations
- Add error handling (toast or alert)

==================================================
DO NOT:
==================================================

- Do NOT touch Cloudinary logic
- Do NOT change authentication core
- Do NOT break navigation
- Do NOT remove any existing feature

==================================================
FINAL TASK
==================================================

After implementation:

1. Ensure app runs without errors
2. Verify:
   - user role saved correctly
   - provider restrictions working
   - request creation works
   - request lists show correctly
   - status updates work

3. Commit changes with message:

"Phase 3.6 + 3.7: roles system + request system stabilization"

4. Push to GitHub remote

==================================================
OUTPUT
==================================================

Return:
- Summary of changes
- Files modified (only list, not full code)
